theorems.name: Elliptic maximum principle
theorems.reduces: control an interior extremum of an elliptic solution by boundary values once the sign and ellipticity hypotheses are checked
verificationHints: check the PDE inequality has the correct sign
check boundary values dominate the claimed bound
check regularity assumptions for the chosen maximum principle
preferredRecipe: identify the elliptic operator and sign convention
avoidPatterns: do not apply the maximum principle before fixing the sign convention for the operator
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_differentiate and wolfram_simplify to verify Lu and boundary inequalities for explicit candidates.

theorems.name: Hopf boundary point lemma
theorems.reduces: turn a nonconstant boundary extremum for an elliptic inequality into a strict sign condition on the outward normal derivative
prerequisites: interior sphere or suitable boundary regularity
verificationHints: check the solution is not constant
check the boundary point satisfies the geometric hypothesis
preferredRecipe: compute or identify the normal derivative sign
avoidPatterns: do not infer the sign of the normal derivative without specifying inward versus outward normal
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_differentiate to compute explicit normal derivatives and wolfram_simplify to check their sign under assumptions.

theorems.name: Sobolev-Poincare inequality
theorems.reduces: bound a function norm by a derivative norm under zero boundary, compact support, or mean-zero hypotheses
verificationHints: check the exponent is in the valid range
track dependence of constants on the domain
preferredRecipe: verify zero trace or mean-zero condition
avoidPatterns: do not use Poincare without a normalization condition
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_integrate to verify explicit norm integrals and wolfram_simplify to compare exponents in model examples.

theorems.name: Calderon-Zygmund elliptic estimate
theorems.reduces: control second derivatives of an elliptic solution by the right-hand side and lower-order norms under ellipticity and regularity hypotheses
prerequisites: coefficient regularity appropriate to the estimate
verificationHints: check whether the estimate is interior or global
track lower-order terms and boundary conditions
avoidPatterns: do not use a global boundary estimate when only an interior estimate is justified
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_differentiate for explicit second derivatives and wolfram_integrate for simple Lp norm checks.

theorems.name: Cauchy-Schwarz inequality
theorems.reduces: bound an inner product or integral product by the product of L2 norms
verificationHints: state equality conditions only when needed
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_integrate for explicit L2 norm integrals and wolfram_simplify for the resulting bound.

theorems.name: Young inequality for products
theorems.reduces: split a product term into absorbable and controlled parts, typically with an epsilon parameter
prerequisites: matching conjugate exponents or quadratic form
verificationHints: check epsilon is chosen small enough to absorb the target term
track dependence of constants on epsilon
avoidPatterns: do not hide epsilon-dependent constants when they matter for iteration or compactness
theorems.wolframHint: Use wolfram_simplify or wolfram_solve to verify algebraic inequalities involving epsilon under positivity assumptions.
